description: In a restaurant, Erika holds Joey's right hand in both of hers near her face, laughing. Joey says, "Hey."
Erika continues laughing, looking at Joey while keeping her hands clasped around his right hand.
Erika kisses Joey's hand. Joey smiles and says, "Like at the end of a dance hey."
Erika kisses Joey's knuckles repeatedly, laughing continuously. Joey looks at her and smiles broadly.
Joey says "Hey" again and chuckles. The scene changes to a library. Phoebe sits holding an acoustic guitar, surrounded by children sitting on the floor.
Phoebe strums the guitar and sings, "There'll be times when you get older when you'll want to sleep with people..."
She continues singing, "...just to make them like you but don't," lifting her right hand from the guitar to point her index finger on "don't."
The adults in the back, including Rachel, Chandler, and Monica, stare with wide eyes.
Phoebe resumes playing and singing, "cause that's another thing that you don't want to do everybody." She moves her head to the rhythm.
The children join in singing, "That's another thing that you don't want to do," while clapping. Phoebe smiles and nods.
Phoebe smiles, claps her hands together, and says "Excellent." The children cheer and clap.
Chandler claps and says, "excellent. Very informative. Not at all inappropriate." Rachel and Monica sit beside him, their expressions remaining still.
Phoebe stands up, holding her guitar by the neck.
Phoebe gestures with her hand and says, "Thank you for coming, everybody. There are cookies in the back." The children stand and move toward the back of the room.
The children walk past Phoebe. Rob approaches Phoebe.
Rob stops in front of Phoebe and says, "That was great."
Phoebe smiles. Rob says, "The kids loved you."
Phoebe grins and adjusts her guitar strap.
Phoebe raises her right fist and says, "Yay. I rock."
Rob holds a small container in his left hand and gestures with his right.
Rob points at her with his right hand and says, "And you know why? Because you told the truth." Phoebe removes her guitar strap.
Rob gestures with his hand and continues, "And nobody ever tells kids the truth. You were incredible." Phoebe smiles and runs her hand through her hair.
Phoebe looks at Rob, her hands raised slightly.
Phoebe says, "But?" Rob tilts his head and asks, "How did you know there was a but?"
Phoebe gestures with both hands and says, "I sensed these things. It was either butt or butter."
Rob looks down briefly, then back up at Phoebe.
Rob gestures with his right hand holding the container and says, "The thing is, I think some of the parents..."
Rob continues, "...they were kind of hoping that you play more songs about, like, barnyard,"
Rob says, "animals." Phoebe says, "I can do that." Rob asks, "Really?"
Phoebe nods and says, "Yeah." Rob smiles and says, "Because that would be fantastic."
Phoebe smiles, looking at Rob.
Phoebe smiles and asks, "What?"
Rob looks at Phoebe.
Rob asks, "You want to kiss me?" Phoebe replies, "I'm thinking about,"
Phoebe and Rob lean in towards each other.
Phoebe says, "it," and they kiss.
They pull away from the kiss and look at each other.
Phoebe says, "Okay. So, if I get a library card, can I take you out sometime?"
The scene changes to a hallway where a young blonde girl stands next to a small table. Monica sits at the table, taking a pink item from a tray.
Monica places the pink item into her black purse. The girl stares at her. Monica looks up at the girl.
Monica gestures with her left hand and says, "What? I'm unemployed."
Monica closes her purse, stands up, and hands a coin to the girl.
The scene changes to a dimly lit room where bats hang upside down in a glass enclosure.
Ross and a Zoo Keeper stand in front of the bat enclosure. The Zoo Keeper looks at the bats and says, "The bat ambassador of darkness..."
The Zoo Keeper continues, "...flitting out of his cave like a winged messenger." Ross stares straight ahead.
The Zoo Keeper continues, "Sightless specter of the macabre," leaning slightly toward Ross.
Ross turns his head toward the Zoo Keeper and holds up a small plastic bag containing orange pieces.
Ross interrupts, "buddy. My monkey." The Zoo Keeper stands up straight and says, "Oh, yeah, right."
The Zoo Keeper leans closer to Ross, lowering his voice.
The Zoo Keeper lightly touches Ross's arm and says softly, "There was a break in a few months back. Inside job."
Ross stares at the Zoo Keeper.
The Zoo Keeper gestures with his hands and says, "Your monkey was taken?"
He holds up two fingers as he continues, "Along with a snowy egret, a two toed sloth and three hooded"
The Zoo Keeper drops his hands and finishes, "sweatshirts from the zoo gift shop." Ross says, "Oh, my God."
Ross gestures with his hands and says, "But the zoo told me my monkey was dead."
The Zoo Keeper raises his eyebrows, grips his broom handle, and asks, "The zoo?"
The Zoo Keeper tilts his head and asks, "You believe everything the zoo tells you?"
Ross nods slightly, his brow furrowed.
Ross says, "That's the only thing the zoo's ever told me."
The Zoo Keeper gestures with his right hand and says, "Of course they're going to say he's dead."
The Zoo Keeper points his finger and says, "They don't want the bad publicity. It's all a great big cover up."
He leans closer to Ross and asks, "Do you have any idea how high up this thing"
The Zoo Keeper finishes, "goes?" Ross points backward with his thumb and says, "That guy lipson."
The Zoo Keeper leans forward, eyes wide, and asks, "Lipson knows?"
Ross grabs the Zoo Keeper's shirt collar with both hands and asks, "Do you have any idea who else knows?"
The Zoo Keeper stammers, "No. I only know"
Ross releases the Zoo Keeper's collar.
The Zoo Keeper says, "Lipson knows." He turns his head toward the right and says, "Huh?"
The Zoo Keeper turns to face an adjacent enclosure.
The scene changes to a possum inside a glass enclosure. A voice says, "Hello, Mr."
The possum crawls over the sand. The voice continues, "Possum."
The scene returns to the Zoo Keeper and Ross.
The Zoo Keeper looks at the possum enclosure and says, "Enigma of the trees upside down denizen of the night taunting gravity"
Ross interrupts, his volume louder, "my monkey. My monkey."
The Zoo Keeper turns back to Ross.
The Zoo Keeper points his finger at Ross and says softly, "Word on the street."
The Zoo Keeper lowers his hand.
The Zoo Keeper gestures with his right hand and says, "Well, when I say street, I mean those little pretend streets they have here at the zoo."
Ross nods and says, "Of course."
Ross stares intently at the Zoo Keeper.

In a dimly lit room with glass animal enclosures, the Zoo Keeper stands holding a broom handle with Ross. The Zoo Keeper looks at Ross and says, "Your monkey found a new career in the entertainment field. That's all I know," shaking his head slightly.
Ross exhales heavily and shifts his gaze away from the Zoo Keeper.
Ross looks back at the Zoo Keeper, shaking his head slightly, and says, "This is unbelievable."
The Zoo Keeper adjusts his grip on the broom handle, bringing it closer to his mouth, and says, "So,"
The Zoo Keeper maintains his gaze on Ross.
The Zoo Keeper rubs his right thumb and fingers together while asking, "what is this information worth to you, my friend?"
Ross stares at the Zoo Keeper with a slight frown.
Ross furrows his eyebrows and asks, "Are you trying to get me to bribe you?"
The Zoo Keeper looks at Ross, his facial expression remaining relaxed.
The Zoo Keeper replies, "Maybe."
Ross tilts his head slightly and gestures with one hand.
Ross says, "But you already told me everything," keeping his gaze fixed on the Zoo Keeper.
The Zoo Keeper slowly lowers the broom handle from his face and stares silently at Ross, his mouth slightly open.
The scene changes to an exterior view of a city skyline of tall buildings across a body of water at dusk.

The view transitions to an outdoor view of a large stone building with a lion statue, then the setting changes to the interior of a room where Chandler, Rachel, Monica, Ross, and Joey stand together, surrounded by a group of seated children.
Ross unrolls and holds up a large poster showing a woman in a swimsuit and a monkey holding a beer bottle. "Check it out," Ross says, "He actually is the monkey."
The text on the poster reads "MONKEYSHINE BEER Cause It's a Jungle Out There." Chandler looks at the poster and says, "Shine, monkey." Monica, standing next to him, adds, "Oh, wow."
Ross lowers the poster slightly.
Monica asks, "Well, so what are you going to do?"
Ross continues, "Well, I guess I'm going to call the beer company and try to find out where he is." Chandler replies, "That's what I did when I lost my client. Sales"
Ross rolls up the poster. The scene changes to Phoebe standing in front of the seated children, holding an acoustic guitar.
Phoebe smiles broadly at the children and says, "okay. Hi again."
The children cheer loudly and respond, "Hi Phoebe!" in unison.
Phoebe adjusts her guitar strap and says, "Today we're going to start with some songs about barnyard animals," maintaining her smile.
Phoebe begins to strum her guitar, looking down at the children. A man stands in the background watching her.
Phoebe sings while playing the guitar, "Oh, the cow in the meadow goes moo oh, the cow in the meadow goes moo then the farmer hits him on the head and"
Phoebe continues singing, "grinds him up and that's how we get hamburgers," keeping her eyes wide and smiling.
Monica and Joey look at each other with wide eyes. Chandler covers his mouth with his hand. Phoebe continues strumming the guitar.
Phoebe smiles broadly and sings loudly, "chicken"
The scene changes to a television screen displaying a hospital room. Joey, wearing teal scrubs and a doctor's coat, stands next to a bed where a woman lies unconscious.

On the TV, another doctor says to Joey's character, "you're the only one who can save her, Drake." Joey's character replies, "Damn it. I'm a doctor. I'm not God." The scene changes to an apartment living room where Ross, Chandler, and Joey watch the television. Chandler says, "Well, there goes my whole belief system."
Three loud knocks are heard on the door as Ross, Chandler, and Joey remain seated in the living room.

Joey jumps up, gesturing rapidly, and says, "It's Erica. My God. Quick, shut off the" Rachel points toward the TV from the kitchen area and interrupts, "wait. I want to see what happens." Joey replies, "I get Leslie out of the coma, and then we"
Joey finishes his sentence, "make" Monica, sitting at the kitchen counter, asks, "well, how can that be?"
Monica continues, "You were just kissing Sabrina," keeping her gaze on Joey.
Rachel turns her head slightly toward Monica and says, "Monica, it's a world where Joey is a neurosurgeon."
Joey quickly walks to the apartment door and opens it, revealing Erika standing in the hallway holding a black bag.
Joey steps back slightly and says, "Hey, Erica. Come on in. How did you get here so fast?"
Erika steps inside, keeping her gaze fixed on Joey, and says, "I just saw you in Salem."
Joey nods quickly.
Joey says, "Right."
Erika gestures with one hand and says, "They choppered me"
Erika takes another step into the apartment.
Erika finishes her sentence, "up," as she stops walking.
Erika looks toward the living room and says, "Oh, and I see you're having a little party, too." She turns sharply back to Joey and asks, "Is she? Huh?" Joey asks, "Who?"
Erika stares directly at Joey and says loudly, "Sabrina. I know about you two. I saw you today kissing in the doctor's lounge."
Ross and Chandler sit silently, watching the interaction. Joey exhales heavily, his shoulders dropping.
Joey steps toward Erika and says, "That's not what you think that was." Erika responds sharply, "You told me I was the only one."
Joey stands sweating heavily, breathing quickly, and looking downward, while Erika stares at him with her hands on her hips.

Joey shakes his head and says, "All right, look, that's it. I don't think we should see each other anymore." He raises his hands with palms open and continues, "All right, look, I know I should have told you this a"
Joey continues speaking, "long time ago, but I am not Drake Ramoray, okay? I'm not even a doctor," moving his hands continuously.
Joey states clearly, "I'm an actor. I just pretend to be a doctor." Erika gasps, presses her hand to her chest, and says loudly, "Oh, my God. Do the people at the hospital know about this?"
Ross and Chandler look at Joey silently. Joey glances quickly toward Monica and Rachel in the kitchen.
Joey asks, "Somebody want to help me out here?" his gaze moving between his friends.
Rachel raises her index finger and says, "Oh, I know."
Rachel quickly walks over to the television set. Erika watches her movement.
On the television screen, the woman in the hospital bed opens her eyes and says softly, "Where am I?"
Joey's character leans over the hospital bed on the television screen.
Joey's character on the TV says softly, "University Hospital."
Joey's character on the TV continues, "The same place you've been for the last 18 years." In the apartment, Rachel points repeatedly at the TV screen.
Erika looks at the television screen, her hand still pressed to her chest, then turns her gaze back to Joey.
Erika asks, "How can you be here in there?" Joey gestures toward the TV and says, "Because it's a television show," Erika frowns slightly and asks, "Drake, what are you getting at?"
Chandler smiles slightly and turns his head toward Ross.
Chandler looks toward Joey and says, "Nice girl."
Chandler adds, "Not great with concepts," shaking his head slightly.
Joey stares forward with a neutral expression.
In an apartment living room, Ross and Chandler sit in chairs. Joey stands nearby with wet patches on his shirt. Joey says, "I'm not Drake," and Ross stands up, adding, "That's right."
Ross turns to Erika, who stands nearby, and says, "He's not Drake."
Ross gestures toward Joey and continues, "He's Hans Ramoray, Drake's evil twin." Joey frowns slightly.
Audience laughter is audible. Joey maintains a confused expression and glances around.
Erika looks at Ross and asks, "Is this"
Erika shifts her gaze to Joey.
Rachel steps forward holding a glass of water and says, "yes."
Rachel points at Joey and adds, "Yes, it is true."
Rachel continues, "And I know this because he pretended to be Drake to sleep with me."
Rachel throws the water from her glass into Joey's face.
The audience gasps and laughs. Joey closes his eyes, exhales, and wipes his face with his hand.
Monica steps forward holding a glass of water.
Monica says, "And then he told me he would run away with me, and he didn't," before throwing her water into Joey's face.
Joey turns his head slightly as audience laughter sounds. Chandler steps forward holding a small container.
Chandler points at Joey and says, "And you let the toilet seat up, you bastard," before throwing water from the container into Joey's face.
Chandler grins and steps back. Joey wipes water from his eyes as audience laughter continues.
Erika steps toward Joey and speaks to him. Joey exhales and looks down.
Joey looks up at Erika.
Joey says, "Yes, I'm afraid it is. You deserve much better than me, Erica. You deserve to be with the"
Joey continues, "real Drake. He's the one you fell in love with. Go to Salem. Find him."
Joey gestures to himself and says, "He's the guy for you."
Erika steps closer to Joey.
Erika says, "Oh, Hans."
Erika throws her arms around Joey and kisses him. Joey embraces her back.
Ross and Chandler call out, "Hans. Hans."
Joey and Erika continue kissing.
Rachel calls out, "Yo, evil twin."
Joey and Erika pull apart.
Joey turns back to Erika and says, "Right. Goodbye, Erica."
Erika steps back and picks up her bag.
Joey says, "Good luck in Salem."
Erika holds her bag and turns toward the door.
Erika looks back at Joey and says, "Take care. I'll never forget you, Hans."
Erika exits through the door. Joey closes it and turns back toward the group, smiling.
Joey points and says, "All right, the people who threw the water." Chandler, Monica, and Rachel raise their hands defensively, and Chandler says, "Helping you out. Helping you out."
The setting changes to a coffee shop. Phoebe sits with Rob and asks, "Fired."
Rob looks at Phoebe.
Phoebe asks, "Why?"
Rob says, "The library board has had a lot of complaints from parents about some of the stuff in your songs."
Phoebe responds, "I can't believe this is the"
Phoebe continues, "library. I mean, books, free speech, newspapers on sticks."
Rob replies, "I know."
Phoebe gestures with her hands and asks, "And what about the kids?"
Phoebe asks, "Did you tell your board about how the kids want to hear the truth?"
Rob shakes his head slightly and says, "No."
Phoebe looks down and then back at Rob.
Phoebe says, "I see."
Rob leans forward slightly.
Rob says, "Maybe if you just played some regular kiddie songs."
Phoebe raises a hand to her chest and says, "No. What do you want me to be? Like some stupid, big, like, purple dinosaur?"
Rob shakes his head.
Rob says, "I'm not saying you have to be Barney." Phoebe asks, "Who's Barney?"
Rob looks at Phoebe. The view shifts outside briefly, showing a bus driving past on a city street.
The setting changes back to the coffee shop. Ross approaches Chandler, who stands near a couch where Monica and Rachel sit.
Ross stops near Chandler. Chandler says, "Believe this? Oh, let me guess. What? What?"
Chandler continues, "Some weebles actually do fall down."
Ross stares at Chandler with a flat expression.
Chandler gestures with one hand and says, "A stitch in time now only saves seven. What do you got?"
Audience laughter sounds. Ross looks away, exhaling.
Ross says, "Never mind," and starts to turn away.
Monica, sitting on the couch, turns to Ross.
Monica asks, "What?"
Ross turns back and says, "Well, I track down Marcel and get this. He's healthy, he's happy, and he's right here in New York filming Outbreak Two. The"
Ross raises his hands and continues, "Virus Takes Manhattan."
Rachel and Monica smile, saying, "You're kidding. Oh, my God, this is amazing."
Joey, sitting nearby, points and says, "I know. I finally get a part on TV, and the monkey's making movies."
The group looks toward a nearby stage area, where Phoebe stands holding a piece of paper and a guitar.
Phoebe looks at her paper and says, "Okay, Rachel, I'm ready." Rachel leans forward and says, "Okay."
Rachel stands up from the couch and walks toward Phoebe.

In a coffee shop, Rachel stands at a microphone holding a piece of paper. Phoebe stands beside her holding a guitar. Ross, Monica, Chandler, and Joey sit on couches. A young boy pushes open the front door and enters.
The boy steps inside, looking toward Rachel and Phoebe. "Excuse me. Is this where the singing lady is who tells the truth?" he asks.
Phoebe raises her right hand slightly.
"Yeah, I guess. That's me," Phoebe says.
The boy runs back to the door, pulls it open, and looks outside.
The boy yells through the open doorway, "She's here," waving his arm to gesture outside.
A large group of children rushes through the door into the coffee shop, walking past Rachel and Phoebe to gather around the couches.
The children sit on the floor and couches around the friends. Phoebe begins playing her guitar.
Phoebe plays her guitar and sings into the microphone, "Sometimes men love women and sometimes men love men and then there are."
Phoebe continues singing, "bisexuals though some just say they're kidding themselves." The children and the friends watch her.
Chandler and Joey sit on a couch, smiling as they watch Phoebe.
Phoebe sings, "la la."
The friends and the children join in singing "La la la la" as Phoebe plays the guitar. Chandler smiles broadly.
The setting changes to a city street with film production equipment. Crew members walk around. Chandler, Joey, Ross, Monica, and Rachel walk together down the street.

Ross walks between Chandler and Monica. He says, "This is so exciting. I haven't seen my monkey in almost a year." Chandler turns his head toward Ross and asks, "What, you never looked down in the shower?"
Ross stops walking and looks at Chandler, his facial expression remaining still.
"Oh, please. I'm not allowed to make one joke in the monkey is penis genre?" Chandler asks. A security guard wearing a black jacket and cap approaches the group.
The guard extends his arms and says, "Come on, people. Back up. Please. Back up. Come."
The guard pushes them back slightly. "on, come on, come. Come on. On," he says. Ross raises his hand and asks, "Excuse me. Where can we find the monkey?"
The guard points and replies, "I'm sorry, guys. Close set."
Ross steps forward slightly and says, "I'm sorry. You don't understand."
"I'm a friend of his. We used to live together," Ross continues.
The guard replies, his pitch rising on the second half of his sentence, "Yeah, and I have a timeshare in the Poconos with Flipper."
The guard turns and walks away. Monica looks down the street and points forward.
Pointing, Monica says, "Ross. There he is. Hey, buddy. Marcel." Ross smiles, waves his hand high, and calls out. A blonde woman walks past carrying a small monkey on her shoulder.
Ross continues to smile and calls out, "Marcel."
The woman and the monkey keep walking past without stopping. Ross lowers his hand, his smile fading.
Ross stares in the direction the monkey went, his face still. Chandler, Joey, Monica, and Rachel look toward him.
Ross starts singing, "In the jungle, the mighty jungle the lion sleeps tonight," bobbing his head slightly to the rhythm. Joey and Chandler look at each other.
Ross continues singing. The blonde woman stops walking and turns back toward the group, the monkey still on her shoulder.
Ross continues singing, "in the jungle, the mighty jungle the lion sleeps tonight," stepping forward slightly. Joey and Chandler watch him.
Joey and Chandler turn toward each other and begin to sing.
Joey and Chandler sing, "A wimoweh."
Joey and Chandler continue chanting rhythmically. Ross continues singing the main melody. The blonde woman smiles, and the monkey looks toward them.
Joey and Chandler sing, "A wimoweh."
Joey and Chandler continue chanting rhythmically, their volume rising. Ross tilts his head back and sings a long, high-pitched vocalization.
The monkey jumps down from the woman's shoulder and runs across the street toward the group. Ross crouches down with open arms.
The monkey climbs onto Ross's shoulder. Ross stands up, smiling broadly, and the monkey settles on his back.
The friends gather around Ross, smiling and reaching out to touch the monkey. Ross laughs.
The view changes to the New York City skyline, then shifts back to the city street. The friends are standing together behind a striped barricade.
The security guard approaches them, pointing. "Excuse me, folks. This is a closed set," he says. Joey replies, "We know, but we're friends with the monkey."
The guard walks past them. Ross approaches from the side, holding a small grey stuffed elephant.
Ross smiles and says, "Good morning."
Holding up the stuffed animal, Ross says, "Hey, pal. Look who brought it to your old friend, Harry Elephante."
The monkey, now sitting on the shoulder of a man wearing a brown scarf, grabs the stuffed elephant from Ross, drops it onto the street, and turns its back.
A blonde crew member walks past and says, "Whoa, dude."
"Burn," she adds, continuing to walk past.
Ross stares at the dropped toy, his smile gone. Chandler bends down to pick it up.
Ross turns to Joey and Chandler and says, "I don't get it."
"He seems so happy to see me yesterday," Ross continues.
The man with the scarf turns to Ross and says, "Hey, don't take it personal. He's under a lot of pressure. You know, starring in a movie and all."
Rachel looks at the man with the scarf.
Rachel asks, "Now, just how big of a star is Marcel?"
The man with the scarf looks at Rachel.
The visual goes black, and the audio stops.
